water.name: Fort Churchill Cooling Pond
aliases: Fort Churchill Cooling Pond Cooperative Management Area

sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — Fishing Seasons CR 25-16 (Jan 1, 2026 – Dec 31, 2027)
sources.quotedText: Fort Churchill Cooling Pond Cooperative Management Area, fishing prohibited from the center dike and posted areas. Season: Second Saturday in February through September 30. Limits — Daily: 5 trout and 15 warmwater game fish of which not more than 2 may be black bass. Minimum size for black bass is 14 inches total length. Possession: 10 trout and 30 warmwater game fish of which not more than 4 may be black bass. Hours: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.

sources.title: Fort Churchill Cooling Pond — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: Fort Churchill Cooling Pond is the primary cooling pond for the steam-generated power plant operated by NV Energy. This is a warmwater fishery with self-sustaining largemouth bass, bluegill, and channel catfish populations; however, trout is stocked during the winter when the power plant operation is suspended and channel catfish is stocked when available. Fishing is only allowed from shore. Stocking Updates: 1034 Rainbow Trout, 7.8 in, 02-12-2026; 1500 Channel Catfish, 4 in, 05-29-2025; 1032 Rainbow Trout, 9.0 in, 02-06-2025; 3000 Channel Catfish, 4.1 in, 05-23-2024; 1136 Rainbow Trout, 9.6 in, 02-07-2024; 983 Rainbow Trout, 9.8 in, 03-24-2023; 1033 Rainbow Trout, 10.1 in, 02-07-2023.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

sources.title: USGS GNIS (Geographic Names Information System) — Domestic Names record for gaz_id 848470, "Fort Churchill Cooling Ponds", Reservoir, Lyon Co., NV (Mason Butte quad)

regulations.humanSummary: Fort Churchill Cooling Pond is open to fishing from the second Saturday in February through September 30.
regulations.verbatimText: Season: Second Saturday in February through September 30.

parameters.counting_rule: Trout limit is separate from warmwater game fish: 5 trout and 15 warmwater game fish daily (10 trout and 30 warmwater game fish in possession), of which not more than 2 warmwater fish daily (4 in possession) may be black bass.

regulations.humanSummary: Daily limit of 5 trout, possession limit of 10 trout.

parameters.counting_rule: Of the 15 daily warmwater game fish (30 in possession), not more than 2 daily (4 in possession) may be black bass, subject to a 14-inch minimum total length.

regulations.humanSummary: Daily limit of 15 warmwater game fish (30 in possession), of which no more than 2 per day (4 in possession) may be black bass.
regulations.verbatimText: 15 warmwater game fish of which not more than 2 may be black bass... Possession: ...30 warmwater game fish of which not more than 4 may be black bass.

regulations.humanSummary: Minimum size for black bass is 14 inches total length.
regulations.verbatimText: Minimum size for black bass is 14 inches total length.

parameters.note: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.

regulations.humanSummary: Fishing hours run from 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.
regulations.verbatimText: Hours: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.

parameters.note: Fishing is prohibited from the center dike and posted areas of the cooperative management area.

regulations.humanSummary: Fishing is prohibited from the center dike and other posted areas of the Fort Churchill Cooling Pond Cooperative Management Area.
regulations.verbatimText: Fort Churchill Cooling Pond Cooperative Management Area, fishing prohibited from the center dike and posted areas.

parameters.note: Standard Nevada resident/nonresident fishing license required for anglers 12 and older.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; no license required under age 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

stockingSchedule.note: NDOW states trout is stocked during the winter when the power plant operation is suspended; recorded stocking dates cluster in February (and once in March).
NDOW states channel catfish is stocked when available, with no fixed schedule.
